creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Robert Clausecker from comment #2)
> It even breaks some of my unit tests (which get confused by the extra
> stderr output).
I understand, but as I've said earlier, then you should rather fix your tests.  You mention "make stage-qa" which is part of our ports infrastructure.  We do not encourage calling naked commands, i.e. you should use ${EGREP} which points to /usr/bin/egrep as defined by Mk/bsd.commands.mk.  If you rely on egrep(1) being in your $PATH which includes 3rd-party programs then you should not assume any particular implementation or behavior [not] kicking in.

> What's the rationale behind this?
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45461653/why-these-variants-of-egrep-fgrep-and-rgrep-are-deprecated

> Do you think they're actually going to remove the egrep and fgrep scripts?
Well yeah, I'd assume that sooner or later they will, otherwise what's the point of the warning?

> My position: if you install the scripts, they should not nag you.
> Otherwise don't install the scripts, that's fine, too with me (as we
> already provide egrep and fgrep in base).  It may not be fine with
> other users though.
It's not that I like the nagging, I just don't like to monkey-patch the vendor code (it would be less of an issue if they e.g. had provided configure switch to disable it), but before we do that (if anything) -- why not just use ${EGREP}?
